user_message: Can you help me prepare the forecast? The customer (Iron Creek Mining) is asking about account health.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 10:42 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining account health

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-02-05 10:27 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 08:48 EST
Subject: account health - Action Required

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: account health Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Iron Creek Mining)

[00:02:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on account health from our last session.

[00:05:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on timeline constraints and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:08:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:10:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:13:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:16:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Priya (Iron Creek Mining): On the account health front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
